question_text: An 72-year-old male with a surgical same-day admission has developed hypotension and oliguria; determine the most appropriate next management step.

gold_labs.label: Lactate
gold_labs.proposed_reason: Elevated lactate can indicate tissue hypoperfusion and poor perfusion status, critical in assessing cardiac function.

guideline_citation.claim: Lactate levels are useful in assessing tissue hypoperfusion and guiding resuscitation efforts.

reference_answer: Initiate fluid resuscitation and consider vasopressor support based on lactate levels and hemodynamic stability.
causal_chain: Elevated lactate indicates poor tissue perfusion -> Poor perfusion suggests inadequate cardiac output -> Initiate fluid resuscitation to improve cardiac output -> Monitor lactate levels and hemodynamics to guide further management.

pubmed_citations.title: A plea for personalization of the hemodynamic management of septic shock.
